1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
31	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
34	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
36	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
37	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
38	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
39	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
40	
41	**Never write to `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` from the loop.** That file is only consumed by the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor execute` command run by the mathematician. The autonomous loop's plan agent must pass refactor directives **inline** in the Agent-tool prompt to the `refactor` subagent — never stage them in a file. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` flow; treat those as historical noise and prune them out when you rewrite those files.
42	
43	## Protected Declarations
44	
45	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
46	
47	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
48	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
49	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
50	
51	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
52	
53	The blueprint uses two active markers.
54	
55	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
56	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
57	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
58	
59	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
60	
61	## User Interaction
62	
63	Users provide hints in two places:
64	
65	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
66	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
67	
68	## Agent Roles
69	
70	### Plan Agent
71	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
72	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
73	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
74	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) via the Agent tool, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering and per-subagent guidance. Pass each directive **inline** in the Agent-tool prompt.
75	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
76	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
77	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
78	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
79	
80	### Prover Agent
81	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
82	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
83	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
84	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
85	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
86	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
87	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
88	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
89	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
90	
91	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
92	
93	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand via the Agent tool, passing the directive inline. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
94	
95	- **`refactor`** — read `.claude/agents/refactor.md` and `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
96	- **`analogy`** — read `.claude/agents/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
97	- **`challenger`** — read `.claude/agents/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
98	
99	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
100	
101	### Review Agent
102	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
103	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
104	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
105	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
106	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
107	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) **update the blueprint chapter markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) to reflect the verified state, and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs. You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts — that is required for marker updates. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint files.**
6	
7	## Iteration number — canonical
8	
9	Your invocation prompt contains both `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. They are different counters and you must keep them straight:
10	
11	- `Archon iteration: NNN` is Archon's authoritative counter — matches `logs/iter-NNN/` and `archon[NNN/phase]` commit messages. Use it when you reference *the iteration this session belongs to* (in `summary.md` metadata) and *the next iteration* (when titling `recommendations.md`, e.g. "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})"). Do not invent your own counter. If older `recommendations.md` files use a different counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the number from your prompt header.
12	- `Session number: M` is independent — it counts prover rounds only. Use it for the directory name (`session_M/`) and nothing else.
13	
14	## Step 1: Identify Context
15	
16	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
17	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
18	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
19	
20	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
21	
22	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
23	
24	The file contains:
25	
26	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
27	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
28	
29	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
30	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
31	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
32	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
33	
34	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
35	
36	## Step 3: Read Recent History
37	
38	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
39	
40	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
41	
42	Create the session folder and write two files:
43	
44	```bash
45	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
46	```
47	
48	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
49	
50	Must include:
51	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
52	- For EACH target attempted:
53	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
54	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
55	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
56	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
57	- Recommendations for next session
58	
59	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
60	
61	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
62	
63	```json
64	{
65	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
66	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
67	  "target": {
68	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
69	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
70	  },
71	  "session": {
72	    "id": "session_N",
73	    "model": "model-name"
74	  },
75	  "findings": {
76	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
77	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
78	  },
79	  "attempts": [
80	    {
81	      "attempt": 1,
82	      "strategy": "what was tried",
83	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
84	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
85	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
86	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
87	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
88	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
89	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
90	    }
91	  ],
92	  "next_steps": "..."
93	}
94	```
95	
96	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
97	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
98	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
99	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
100	
101	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
102	
103	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
104	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
105	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
106	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
107	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
108	
109	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md
110	
111	Update (or create) `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`:
112	
113	```markdown
114	# Project Status
115	
116	## Overall Progress
117	- **Total sorry**: <N>
118	- **Solved this session**: <list with file + theorem>
119	- **Partial**: <list with progress summary>
120	- **Blocked**: <list with reasons>
121	- **Untouched**: <list>
122	
123	## Knowledge Base
124	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
125	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
126	
127	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
128	- <target>: <reason>
129	
130	## Last Updated
131	<ISO timestamp>
132	```
133	
134	## Step 6: Update Blueprint Markers
135	
136	You are the **only** agent that writes blueprint markers. The prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) intentionally do not touch the blueprint — they only report what they did in `task_results/<file>.md`. Your job is to translate those reports into accurate markers.
137	
138	For every declaration the prover session touched, decide which marker (if any) belongs on its blueprint block, then edit the relevant chapter file in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`.
139	
140	### Marker rules
141	
142	- `\leanok` inside `\begin{theorem}` / `\begin{lemma}` / `\begin{definition}` — the **statement** is formalized. Add this once the corresponding Lean declaration exists and the file compiles (even if the proof body is `sorry`).
143	- `\leanok` inside `\begin{proof}` — the **proof** is fully formalized. Add this only after verifying with `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lake env lean <file>` that the declaration has **no `sorry`, no `axiom`, no compilation errors**.
144	- `\mathlibok` inside the statement block — the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export, alias, or direct reference. Add this when the prover's task result says the declaration is Mathlib-backed. No `\leanok` on the proof block is expected for Mathlib-backed declarations.
145	- **No marker** — the block is unformalized or the informal statement did not translate cleanly. Leave unmarked and add a `% NOTE: <reason>` comment so the plan agent sees the obstacle.
146	
147	### What to verify before marking
148	
149	- **Statement `\leanok`**: confirm the Lean declaration exists at the name given by `\lean{...}` and the file compiles.
150	- **Proof `\leanok`**: confirm the declaration has no `sorry`, no new `axiom`, no errors. If the prover's report claimed polish-complete but the file still has a `sorry`, do NOT add the proof `\leanok` — trust verification over self-reports.
151	- **`\mathlibok`**: confirm the Lean side references the Mathlib name (e.g. `def foo := Mathlib.bar` or a simple re-export) and does not itself contain a `sorry` or new definition requiring proof.
152	
153	### `\lean{...}` macro maintenance
154	
155	If a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
156	
157	### Record what you marked
158	
159	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing every marker change:
160	
161	```markdown
162	## Blueprint markers updated
163	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `\leanok` to statement (declaration exists, compiles)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `\leanok` to proof (0 sorries, compiles, no axioms)
165	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: left unmarked — prover reported could-not-formalize
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Rules:**
175	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
176	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
177	
178	## Step 7: Self-Validation
179	
180	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
181	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
182	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
183	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
184	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
185	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
186	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
187	- [ ] For every declaration solved or polished this session, the blueprint chapter has the correct marker (`\leanok` on the proof block once the file compiles with no `sorry`; `\mathlibok` if backed by Mathlib; nothing otherwise)
188	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
189	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains in any chapter
190	
191	## Permissions
192	
193	You may write to:
194	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
195	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
196	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.
197	
198	You must NOT write to:
199	- Any `.lean` files
200	- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
201	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
202	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.

1	\chapter{Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence for $\ModuleCat k$-valued sheaf cohomology}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_MayerVietoris}
3	
4	This chapter extracts (iter-027 split) the Mayer-Vietoris LES infrastructure for $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$-valued cohomology, mirroring Mathlib's \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean} and \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean} line-by-line for the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor. The carrier definitions $\HModule$, $\HModulePrime$, etc., live in Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK} and are imported.
5	
6	The build-out is the cohort iter-016 $\to$ iter-026: functorial wrappers (iter-016), LES building blocks $\toBiprod$ and $\fromBiprod$ (iter-017), the composition-is-zero lemma (iter-018), the short-complex carrier and pushout-of-free-sheaves analog (iter-019), the short-exact infrastructure (iter-020), the connecting hom $\delta$ (iter-021), the LES sequence as a $\ComposableArrows$ (iter-022), the technical heart (4 auxiliary lemmas + comparison iso to $\Ext.\contravariantSequence$, iter-023), and the exactness theorem with $\delta$-zero $\mathtt{simp}$ companions (iter-026).
7	
8	\section{Functorial wrappers: cohomology of an open as a presheaf (iter-016)}
9	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
10	
11	After Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime} and Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_zero_linearEquiv} the cohomology-of-an-open carrier $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ is well-defined together with its degree-zero $H^0$ identification with a Hom group. The next natural step is the \emph{functoriality of the carrier in the open variable $X$}: assembling $X \mapsto \HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ into a presheaf
12	\[
13	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n \;\colon\; \Cop \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
14	\]
15	together with the bifunctorial form (varying $F$ as well)
16	\[
17	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;\colon\; \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k) \;\Longrightarrow\; (\Cop \;\Longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat).
18	\]
19	This mirrors Mathlib's $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}$ and $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}$ (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean} L90--101) for the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor.
20	
21	\subsection{Mathlib gap and codomain}
22	
23	A Mathlib survey shows that Mathlib's $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}$ has body
24	\[
25	  \Functor.\mathtt{flip}\bigl((\Functor.\mathtt{op}\,(\yoneda \circ (\Functor.\whiskeringRight\,\_\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{obj}\,\AddCommGrpCat.\mathtt{free} \circ \mathtt{presheafToSheaf}\,\_\,\_)) \circ \mathtt{extFunctor}\,n\bigr)
26	\]
27	and codomain $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat \Longrightarrow (\Cop \Longrightarrow \AddCommGrpCat)$. There is no $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor of this in Mathlib. Mirroring the body verbatim with $\AddCommGrpCat.\mathtt{free} \rightarrow \ModuleCat.\mathtt{free}\,k$ and starting from $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$-valued sheaves yields
28	\[
29	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;:=\; \Functor.\mathtt{flip}\bigl(((\yoneda \circ (\Functor.\whiskeringRight\,\_\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{obj}\,(\ModuleCat.\mathtt{free}\,k) \circ \mathtt{presheafToSheaf}\,J\,\_).\mathtt{op}) \circ \mathtt{extFunctor}\,n\bigr).
30	\]
31	The \emph{codomain remains $\AddCommGrpCat$}, not $\ModuleCat\,k$, because Mathlib's $\Abelian.\mathtt{extFunctor}\,n \,\colon\, \Cop \Longrightarrow C \Longrightarrow \AddCommGrpCat$ always lands in $\AddCommGrpCat$, regardless of which $\Linear k$-enriched abelian category we start in. (A genuinely $\ModuleCat\,k$-valued analog would require a new $\mathtt{linearExtFunctor}$ that Mathlib does not have; we do not introduce it here.) The per-fiber $\Module\,k$ structure on the evaluation $(\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,X)$ is preserved through the iter-014 $\HModulePrime$ reducibility: the underlying $\Type\,u$ matches $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ definitionally (probe-confirmed \lstinline{rfl}), and that carries the $\Module\,k$ inherited from $\Abelian.\Ext.\mathtt{instModule}$ via the $\Linear\,k$ enrichment.
32	
33	\subsection{Why this is the natural iter-016 target}
34	
35	The Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem (Mathlib \texttt{MayerVietoris.lean}) constructs maps of the form $(F.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{ij}.\mathtt{op}$ — restriction maps on cohomology induced by morphisms of opens. A $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavored LES therefore needs the analogous contravariant functoriality of $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ in $X$. Iter-016 lands this functoriality as the smallest probe-confirmable single-iteration unit; iter-017+ then writes the LES theorem directly, reusing $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n$ for the maps.
36	
37	\begin{definition}[Cohomology presheaf functor (\texorpdfstring{$\HModulePrime$}{HModule'} flavor, iter-016)]
38	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheafFunctor}
39	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor}
40	  \leanok
41	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime, thm:HasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK, thm:HasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK}
42	  Let $k$ be a field, $C$ a category with $J : \mathrm{GrothendieckTopology}\,C$, and assume $\HasWeakSheafify\,J\,(\mathrm{Type}\,u)$, $\HasSheafify\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$, and $\HasExt(\Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k))$. For $n : \mathbb N$ define
43	  \[
44	    \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;\colon\; \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k) \;\Longrightarrow\; (\Cop \;\Longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat)
45	  \]
46	  by
47	  \[
48	    \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;:=\; \Functor.\mathtt{flip}\bigl(((\yoneda \circ (\Functor.\whiskeringRight\,\_\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{obj}\,(\ModuleCat.\mathtt{free}\,k) \circ \mathtt{presheafToSheaf}\,\_\,\_).\mathtt{op}) \circ \mathtt{extFunctor}\,n\bigr).
49	  \]
50	\end{definition}
51	
52	\begin{proof}
53	  \leanok
